title: Mosquito Alert Modeling Pipeline
text: vignette walks end--end Mosquito Alert modeling pipeline implemented vectormodelR package. covers required inputs, intermediate artifacts, helper functions, file naming conventions, final reporting step can reproduce adapt workflow new location. canonical pipeline generates feature-enriched dataset, fits Bernoulli occupancy model brms, renders diagnostic report: sections unpack component, provide implementation notes, highlight files written disk.
code: library(vectormodelR)  # Location configuration iso3 <- "ITA" admin_level <- 3 admin_name <- "Roma"  # Model runtime options nchains <- 4 threads_per_chain <- 2  # External data locations/keys (examples) ecmwfr_key <- Sys.getenv("ECMWFR_KEY") raw_landcover <- "data/raw/landcover_italy.tif" raw_ndvi <- "data/raw/ndvi_italy.tif" raw_pop_density <- "data/raw/worldpop_italy.tif"  get_era5_data(   country_iso3 = iso3,   start_year   = 2014,   end_year     = 2025,   ecmwfr_key   = ecmwfr_key,   write_key    = TRUE,   data_format  = "grib",   verbose      = TRUE )  compile_era5_data_v2(   iso3     = iso3,   recent_n = 12,   verbose  = TRUE )  process_era5_data(   iso3             = iso3,   admin_level      = admin_level,   admin_name       = admin_name,   aggregation_unit = "cell" )  map <- get_gadm_data(   iso3      = iso3,   name      = admin_name,   level     = admin_level,   perimeter = TRUE,   rds       = TRUE )  hex_grid <- build_spatial_grid(   iso3        = iso3,   admin_level = admin_level,   admin_name  = admin_name,   shape       = "hex",   write       = TRUE )  processed_lc <- process_landcover_data(   terra::rast(raw_landcover),   iso3        = iso3,   admin_level = admin_level,   admin_name  = admin_name,   proc_dir    = "data/proc",   verbose     = TRUE )  elevation <- get_elevation_data(   iso3       = iso3,   level      = admin_level,   name_value = admin_name )  processed_ndvi <- process_ndvi_data(   terra::rast(raw_ndvi),   iso3,   admin_level,   admin_name )  processed_pop_density <- process_popdensity_data(   terra::rast(raw_pop_density),   iso3,   admin_level,   admin_name )  initialized_data <- initialize_ma_dataset(iso3, admin_level, admin_name)  enriched_data <- add_features(   iso3,   admin_level,   admin_name,   "se,el,ndvi,pd,wx,lc,hex",   verbose = TRUE )  brms_model <- run_brms_model(   enriched_data,   nchains           = nchains,   threads_per_chain = threads_per_chain,   backend           = "cmdstanr",   iso3              = iso3,   admin_level       = admin_level,   admin_name        = admin_name,   write_output      = TRUE,   output_path       = "data/proc",   verbose           = TRUE )  interpret_brms_model(brms_model)

title: Mosquito Alert Modeling Pipeline
text: helpers default storing outputs data/proc. Filenames follow slug produced build_location_identifiers(iso3, admin_level, admin_name): Administrative boundary: spatial_<slug>_adm.Rds Spatial grid: spatial_<slug>_<shape>_grid_<cellsize>.Rds Feature-prepared dataset: model_prep_<slug>_<suffixes>.Rds Fitted model: model_<slug>_brms.Rds Diagnostic report: model_<slug>_brms_report.<ext> Keep raw rasters (land cover, NDVI, population density) data/raw. can customize directories via function arguments (data_dir, proc_dir, output_path) needed.

title: Mosquito Alert Modeling Pipeline
text: get_era5_data() downloads hourly ERA5 fields using ECMWF Web API, storing GRIB files data/raw/era5. compile_era5_data_v2() mosaics tiles, clones latest recent_n months, caches intermediate NetCDF/Parquet files data/proc/era5. process_era5_data() aggregates weather summaries cell hex spatial units, producing statistics like daily maximum temperature 24-hour precipitation totals align modeling features. Make sure ECMWF key registered available running steps. Re-running downloads new months data already exists.

title: Mosquito Alert Modeling Pipeline
text: get_gadm_data() retrieves polygons GADM (reuses cached files). Setting perimeter = TRUE simplifies geometries, rds = TRUE ensures result written spatial_<slug>_adm.Rds. map already loaded, pass via map avoid refetching.

title: Mosquito Alert Modeling Pipeline
text: build_spatial_grid() generates modeling lattice. Supply either map ISO inputs; function automatically reads spatial_<slug>_adm.Rds. Key parameters: cellsize_m: grid spacing meters clip: intersect hexes/squares boundary (default TRUE) shape: choose “hex” “square” write = TRUE, grid saved spatial_<slug>_<shape>_grid_<cellsize>.Rds inside data/proc. saved object sf tibble containing grid_id, centroid coordinates, geometry column.

title: Mosquito Alert Modeling Pipeline
text: process_landcover_data() reprojects, crops, tabulates land-cover classes. writes cleaned rasters data/proc/landcover produces per-grid coverage proportions consumed later add_landcover_features().

title: Mosquito Alert Modeling Pipeline
text: get_elevation_data() downloads elevation tiles, mosaics , derives summary statistics (mean, min, max) grid cell. Outputs live data/proc/elevation.

title: Mosquito Alert Modeling Pipeline
text: process_ndvi_data() ingests NDVI composites, computing proximity distances vegetation (ndvi_ldist_m) summary statistics (ndvi_mean, ndvi_sd). function returns processed rasters per-grid tables ready joins.

title: Mosquito Alert Modeling Pipeline
text: process_popdensity_data() harmonizes WorldPop-style rasters, rescales density units, aggregates counts grid (popdensity_km2). processor respects slug-based naming downstream helpers can locate correct files automatically.

title: Mosquito Alert Modeling Pipeline
text: initialize_ma_dataset() combines Mosquito Alert reports spatial indices: Reads base dataset (model_prep_<slug>_base.Rds). Joins grid identifiers centroids. Adds temporal features (date, year, biweek, day_of_year). Records metadata location_slug output_path later steps.

title: Mosquito Alert Modeling Pipeline
text: add_features() applies enrichment helpers sequence. Supported codes: se: seasonality pseudoabsence indicators el: elevation summaries ndvi: NDVI proximity metrics pd: population-density gradients wx: ERA5 weather summaries lc: land-cover proportions hex / hex_<cellsize>: geometry columns (grid_lon, grid_lat, geometry) Helpers update dataset metadata; final helper writes enriched frame path stored output_path (example, model_prep_<slug>_base_el_ndvi_pd_wx_lc_hex400.Rds). Check attr(enriched_data, "output_path") confirm destination.

title: Mosquito Alert Modeling Pipeline
text: run_brms_model() fits baseline Bernoulli occupancy model. Internally : Validates required columns scales covariates (maxTM_z, ppt_z, ndvi_z, elev_z, pop_z). Builds formula cyclic smooth day_of_year, smooth maximum temperature, linear effects fixed predictors, random intercepts year landcover_code. Calls brms::brm() using requested backend (cmdstanr default). Stores metadata (slug, output path, source dataset) fitted object. Set write_output = TRUE provide output_path persist model model_<slug>_brms.Rds. Adjust priors, iter, sampler controls suit analysis.

title: Mosquito Alert Modeling Pipeline
text: interpret_brms_model() renders diagnostic report includes: Metadata sampler diagnostics (R-hat, effective sample sizes, divergences) Tables fixed, random, smooth terms Conditional effect plots constructed stored prediction grids Land-cover random-effect summaries Neutral narrative text describing parameter estimates Reports default HTML (model_<slug>_brms_report.html) PDF/Word outputs available via format argument.

title: Mosquito Alert Modeling Pipeline
text: Wrap pipeline scripts targets plans iterate multiple locations. Cache heavy rasters data/proc avoid repetitive processing. Set verbose = TRUE log every file gets written. Use build_location_identifiers() custom helpers preserve naming consistency. Explore posterior draws posterior bayesplot deeper diagnostics model fit.

title: An R toolkit for sourcing global data and using it to generate mosquito various models
text: goal VectorModelR provide set tools accessing global datasets, combining Mosquito Alert citizen science data generate Vector Models (https://www.mosquitoalert.com/), including Mosquito Alert citizen science data, smart trap data Irideon Senscape API, traditional mosquito trap data. IMPORTANT: package early stage development, may introduce “breaking” changes. addition, please note package contains functions working Irideon Senscape API, developed Irideon official Irideon package.

title: An R toolkit for sourcing global data and using it to generate mosquito various models
text: can install development version VectorModelR GitHub follows:
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("Mosquito-Alert/vectormodelR")

title: An R toolkit for sourcing global data and using it to generate mosquito various models
text: following example demonstrates complete workflow acquiring data, processing environmental covariates, fitting Bayesian spatial model.

title: An R toolkit for sourcing global data and using it to generate mosquito various models
text: First, load package define target area modeling parameters.
code: library(vectormodelR) library(terra) library(dplyr)  # targeted region target_country_iso3 <- "ESP" target_level        <- 2 target_city         <- "Barcelona"  # modelling parameters nchains             <- 4 threads_per_chain   <- 1

title: An R toolkit for sourcing global data and using it to generate mosquito various models
text: Download vector surveillance data environmental covariates.
code: # Get vector counts from Mosquito Alert and GBIF counts <- vectormodelR::get_vector_counts(   iso3 = target_country_iso3,    level = target_level )  # Inspect available administrative unit names # vectormodelR::get_gadm_names( #   country = target_country,  #   level = target_level,  #   view = "datatable" # )  # Administrative boundaries map <- vectormodelR::get_gadm_data(   iso3 = target_country_iso3,    name = target_city,    level = target_level,    perimeter = TRUE,    rds = TRUE )  # Download ERA5 weather data (requires ECMWF API key) # See ?ecmwfr::wf_set_key for setup instructions # vectormodelR::get_era5_data( #   country_iso3 = target_country_iso3, #   start_year = 2020,  #   end_year = 2021, #   write_key = TRUE # )  # Compile downloaded ERA5 data # vectormodelR::compile_era5_data_v2( #   iso3 = target_country_iso3, #   recent_n = 12, #   verbose = TRUE # )

title: An R toolkit for sourcing global data and using it to generate mosquito various models
text: Build hexagonal grid process environmental layers (Landcover, Elevation, NDVI, Population).
code: # Create hexagonal grids at different resolutions hex_grid400 <- vectormodelR::build_spatial_grid(   iso3 = target_country_iso3,    admin_level = target_level,    admin_name = target_city,   cellsize_m = 400 )  hex_grid800 <- vectormodelR::build_spatial_grid(   iso3 = target_country_iso3,    admin_level = target_level,    admin_name = target_city,    cellsize_m = 800 )  hex_grid1200 <- vectormodelR::build_spatial_grid(   iso3 = target_country_iso3,    admin_level = target_level,    admin_name = target_city,    cellsize_m = 1200 )  # Process ERA5 data (if downloaded) # vectormodelR::process_era5_data( #   iso3 = target_country_iso3, #   admin_level = target_level, #   admin_name = target_city, #   aggregation_unit = "cell" # )  # Process Landcover (requires raw raster input) # lc <- terra::rast("path/to/landcover.tif") # processed_lc = vectormodelR::process_landcover_data(  #   lc, #   iso3 = target_country_iso3, #   admin_level = target_level, #   admin_name = target_city, #   proc_dir = "data/proc" # )  # Get Elevation elevation <- vectormodelR::get_elevation_data(   target_country_iso3,    level = target_level,    name_value = target_city )

title: An R toolkit for sourcing global data and using it to generate mosquito various models
text: Initialize dataset structure add processed features.
code: # Initialize empty dataset structure initialized_data = vectormodelR::initialize_vector_dataset(   iso3 = target_country_iso3,    admin_level = target_level,   admin_name = target_city )  # Add features (Population, Weather, NDVI, etc.) enriched_data = vectormodelR::add_features(   target_country_iso3,   target_level,   target_city,   vector_sources = c("malert", "gbif"),   features = "se,el,ndvi,pd,wx,lc,hex,hex_800,hex_1200" )

what: Sequentially enrich Mosquito Alert model-preparation datasets — add_features
title: Sequentially enrich Mosquito Alert model-preparation datasets — add_features
text: Convenience wrapper locates base model_prep_*_base.Rds file given location applies one enrichment helpers (hex-grid IDs, weather, landcover, NDVI, elevation, population density, pseudoabsences) order supplied. Helpers run sequentially, last step persisting enriched dataset alongside base inputs. Intermediate helpers update metadata (notably output_path attribute) suffixes accumulate expected.

title: Sequentially enrich Mosquito Alert model-preparation datasets — add_features

title: Sequentially enrich Mosquito Alert model-preparation datasets — add_features
text: iso3 Three-letter ISO3 country code. admin_level Administrative level used preparing inputs. admin_name Administrative unit name used file naming scheme. features Character vector (comma-separated string) indicating feature steps run. Accepted values: "hex", "hex_<cellsize>" (example hex_800), "wx"/"weather", "lc"/"landcover", "ndvi", "el"/"elevation", "pd"/"popdensity", "se"/"pseudoabsence". vector_sources Character vector (comma-separated string) describing vector data sources used build base dataset. Accepted values: "malert", "gbif". Determines filename suffix located. data_dir Directory containing model-preparation datasets derived artefacts. Defaults "data/proc". verbose Logical; TRUE, prints progress messages processing. grid_cellsize_m Numeric cell size (meters) corresponding stored hex grid. Defaults 400.

title: Sequentially enrich Mosquito Alert model-preparation datasets — add_features

Attributes source dataset preserved augmented ndvi_source, ndvi_threshold, output_path metadata. ndvi_distance_m – meters report closest raster cell whose NDVI ≥ threshold. ndvi_value_nearest – NDVI value nearest qualifying cell. ndvi_ddf_proximity – optional exponential decay score exp(-α · distance^β); higher values mean nearer high-NDVI areas.

title: Generate pseudoabsences using TRS effort (Mosquito Alert) and TGB (GBIF) — add_pseudoabsences_se
text: dataset Either -memory modelling dataset path corresponding RDS file. data_dir Directory holding processed datasets (TRS/TGB artefacts) pseudoabsence dataset written. Defaults "data/proc". sampling_factor_ma Mosquito Alert pseudoabsences per MA presence (default 10). sampling_factor_gbif GBIF pseudoabsences per GBIF presence (default 10). date_col Name date column shared presence effort tables (default "date"). lon_col Name longitude column presence dataset (default "lon"). lat_col Name latitude column presence dataset (default "lat"). source_col Name data-source column presence dataset (default "source"). se_col Name TRS sampling-effort column (default "SE_expected"). tgb_col Name GBIF target-group background weight column (default "tgb_w"). ma_source Label used Mosquito Alert records source_col (default "malert"). gbif_source Label used GBIF records source_col (default "gbif"). write_output Logical; TRUE (default) persists combined presence+pseudoabsence dataset disk.

what: Build adjacency matrix for a grid (global-safe) — build_grid_adjacency
title: Build adjacency matrix for a grid (global-safe) — build_grid_adjacency
text: Reads polygon grid (spatial_*_hex_grid_<cellsize>.Rds) prepared model data (model_prep_*_wx_lc_ndvi_elev.Rds), keeps cells present model data, reprojects (needed) locally appropriate projected CRS, returns binary queen-contiguity adjacency matrix suitable brms::car(). function first searches size-specific grid filenames falls back legacy names without <cellsize> token.

title: Build adjacency matrix for a grid (global-safe) — build_grid_adjacency
code: build_grid_adjacency(   iso3,   admin_level,   admin_name,   data_dir = "data/proc",   cellsize_m = 400,   target_crs = NULL,   sparse = TRUE,   model )

title: Build adjacency matrix for a grid (global-safe) — build_grid_adjacency
text: iso3 Three-letter ISO3 country code used slug. admin_level Administrative level tied boundary/grid slug. admin_name Administrative unit name used file naming scheme. data_dir Directory holding processed outputs. Defaults "data/proc". cellsize_m Numeric cell size (meters) hex grid file name. Defaults 400. Use values matching build_spatial_grid() output (example, setting cellsize_m = 800 targets spatial_*_hex_grid_800.Rds corresponding grid_id_800 column). target_crs Optional. EPSG code sf::st_crs() object. NULL, suitable CRS chosen automatically (UTM input lon/lat). sparse Logical. TRUE (default), return sparse Matrix. model Either -memory model dataset path prepared model_prep_* RDS file. must supplied; function longer derives model path automatically.

title: Build adjacency matrix for a grid (global-safe) — build_grid_adjacency
text: adjacency matrix (dense base matrix sparse = FALSE, otherwise Matrix::dgCMatrix) row/col names equal size-specific grid identifier (example grid_id_400).

what: Build a regular grid for a supplied polygon — build_spatial_grid
title: Build a regular grid for a supplied polygon — build_spatial_grid
text: Creates tessellation (hexagonal square) given cell size (meters), optionally clips grid polygon footprint, returns grid desired CRS. Uses convert_to_utm() pick appropriate metric projection.

title: Build a regular grid for a supplied polygon — build_spatial_grid
code: build_spatial_grid(   map = NULL,   cellsize_m = 400,   clip = TRUE,   return_crs = 4326,   shape = c("hex", "square"),   write = TRUE,   iso3 = NULL,   admin_level = NULL,   admin_name = NULL,   data_dir = "data/proc" )

title: Build a regular grid for a supplied polygon — build_spatial_grid
text: map Optional sf object describing polygon(s) cover. cellsize_m Numeric. Grid cell size meters (default 400). clip Logical. TRUE (default), intersect hexes polygon’s outline. return_crs Coordinate reference system output (default 4326). shape Character. "hex" (default) hexagons "square" squares. write Logical. TRUE, saves generated grid data_dir using slug-based filename (example spatial_<slug>_hex_grid_400.Rds). iso3 Optional three-letter ISO3 code used admin_level admin_name locate boundary files map supplied. admin_level Optional administrative level corresponding boundary file map supplied. admin_name Optional administrative unit name corresponding boundary file map supplied. data_dir Directory boundary inputs grid outputs stored. Defaults "data/proc".

title: Build a regular grid for a supplied polygon — build_spatial_grid
text: sf object polygons grid_id column. size-specific grid_id_<cellsize> column also added support storing multiple grid resolutions side--side.

title: Build a regular grid for a supplied polygon — build_spatial_grid
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(sf)  # Example polygon (Barcelona bbox) bcn_poly <- st_as_sfc(st_bbox(c(   xmin = 2.05, xmax = 2.25,   ymin = 41.30, ymax = 41.50 )), crs = 4326)  # Hexagonal grid at 400 m cells, returning sf object hex_grid <- build_spatial_grid(bcn_poly, cellsize_m = 400)  # Square grid at 1 km cells, saving to disk sq_grid <- build_spatial_grid(   map = bcn_poly,   cellsize_m = 1000,   shape = "square",   write = FALSE )  # Using stored administrative boundaries (requires matching RDS file) hex_grid <- build_spatial_grid(   iso3 = "ESP",   admin_level = 2,   admin_name = "Barcelona",   write = TRUE ) } # }

text: country_iso3 character. ISO3 country code (e.g., "BGD", "ESP", "USA"). Takes precedence bounding_box. bounding_box numeric(4). c(north, west, south, east) decimal degrees. Ignored country_iso3 provided. output_dir character. Directory downloaded files saved. Default NULL uses "data/weather/grib/" country_iso3 supplied. dataset character. One "reanalysis-era5-single-levels" "reanalysis-era5-land". variables character(). ERA5 variable short names. Default common surface vars. start_year integer. Starting year. Otherwise requires pre-existing keyring entry. ecmwfr_user character. Keyring label use ecmwfr. Default "ecmwfr". write_key logical. Persist ecmwfr_key keyring (default public packages). data_format character. "grib" "netcdf". Default "grib". hours character(). Hours like "00:00"…"23:00". Default 24 hours. skip_if_exists_mb numeric. Skip downloads existing file larger size (MB). Default 1. retry integer. Retries per file transient errors. Default 2. pause_sec numeric. Seconds wait retries. Default 10. verbose logical. Pass wf_request(verbose=). Default FALSE.

title: Retrieve GADM polygons using geodata::gadm() — get_gadm_data
text: iso3 Character. ISO3 country code (e.g., "ESP"). level Integer. GADM administrative level (0 = country, 1 = region, 2 = province, ...). name Character NULL. Optional name vector names filter . Matching defaults case-insensitive substring search can changed via match_type. path Character. Directory caching downloaded data. Default "data/gadm". rds Logical. what: Download GBIF occurrence data for a taxon and country — get_gbif_data
title: Download GBIF occurrence data for a taxon and country — get_gbif_data
text: Submits authenticated GBIF download request given species country, waits completion, retrieves zipped archive, removes downloaded file import, clips occurrences supplied perimeter, returns parsed occurrence records. GBIF credentials can provided via arguments pulled GBIF_USER, GBIF_PWD, GBIF_EMAIL environment variables.

title: Download GBIF occurrence data for a taxon and country — get_gbif_data
code: get_gbif_data(   taxon_key,   iso3,   admin_level,   admin_name,   year_min = NULL,   year_max = NULL,   desired_cols = NULL,   out_dir = "data/proc",   perimeter_dir = "data/proc",   gbif_user = Sys.getenv("GBIF_USER", unset = NA_character_),   gbif_pwd = Sys.getenv("GBIF_PWD", unset = NA_character_),   gbif_email = Sys.getenv("GBIF_EMAIL", unset = NA_character_),   clip_to_perimeter = TRUE,   save_outputs = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Download GBIF occurrence data for a taxon and country — get_gbif_data
text: taxon_key Numeric vector GBIF taxon keys (e.g. c(1651430, 1651891) Aedes albopictus Aedes aegypti). iso3 Three-letter ISO country code (e.g. "ESP"). converted GBIF-required ISO2 code using countrycode. admin_level Administrative level used perimeter generated. clip_to_perimeter = FALSE, value optional. admin_name Administrative unit name used perimeter. clip_to_perimeter = FALSE, value optional. year_min Optional minimum occurrence year. year_max Optional maximum occurrence year. desired_cols Optional character vector (list) column names retain perimeter filtering. Matching case-insensitive accepts common typos occurrenceStatus/occurrenceID. NULL (default), columns preserved. out_dir Directory raw GBIF download perimeter-clipped table saved. Defaults "data/vector". unfiltered results written vector_<iso3>_gbif.Rds; perimeter-clipped output becomes vector_<iso3>_<admin_level>_<admin_name>_gbif.Rds (administrative name normalised lowercase underscores). perimeter_dir Directory containing perimeter RDS artefacts. function looks spatial_<slug>_perimeter.rds location. Ignored clip_to_perimeter = FALSE. gbif_user GBIF username. Defaults Sys.getenv("GBIF_USER"). gbif_pwd GBIF password. Defaults Sys.getenv("GBIF_PWD"). gbif_email GBIF-registered email. Defaults Sys.getenv("GBIF_EMAIL"). clip_to_perimeter Logical; TRUE (default) download intersected administrative perimeter. Set FALSE retain countrywide results without requiring perimeter artefacts. save_outputs Logical; TRUE (default) raw clipped datasets written disk. Use FALSE return data memory . verbose Logical; TRUE (default) progress messages printed.

title: Download GBIF occurrence data for a taxon and country — get_gbif_data
text: Tibble occurrence records. Metadata attributes include GBIF download key (gbif_key), perimeter-clipped table path (gbif_table_path), raw unfiltered table path (gbif_raw_table_path), location slug (location_slug), perimeter source path (perimeter_source).

what: Build daily & lagged weather features from processed ERA5 CSVs — process_era5_data
title: Build daily & lagged weather features from processed ERA5 CSVs — process_era5_data
text: Reads monthly CSV.GZ files created ERA5 compiler (one file per month multiple variables named like era5_<iso3>_YYYY_MM_all_variables.csv.gz), clips GADM admin polygon, aggregates hourly area means, derives daily summaries rolling-window features, saves RDS outputs informative names.

title: Build daily & lagged weather features from processed ERA5 CSVs — process_era5_data
code: process_era5_data(   processed_dir = NULL,   iso3,   admin_level,   admin_name,   out_dir = "data/proc",   start_date = NULL,   end_date = NULL,   wind_calm_kmh = 6,   round_ll = 3,   verbose = TRUE,   attach_to_global = FALSE,   aggregation_unit = c("region", "cell", "hourly"),   polygon_buffer_km = 10 )

title: Build daily & lagged weather features from processed ERA5 CSVs — process_era5_data
text: processed_dir Character. Root dir containing processed/YYYY/era5_<iso3>_YYYY_MM_all_variables.csv.gz. NULL, defaults file.path("data/weather/grib", tolower(iso3), "processed"). iso3 Character. ISO3 code country (e.g., "ESP"). admin_level Integer. GADM administrative level (0=country, 1=region, 2=province, ...). admin_name Character NULL. Exact NAME_<level> match (e.g., "Barcelona"). NULL, whole level geometry used (unioned). out_dir Character. Directory write RDS outputs. Created missing. start_date Date. Earliest date include (UTC). Default "2014-01-01". end_date Date. Latest date include (UTC). Default today. wind_calm_kmh Numeric. Calm-wind threshold km/h MWI logic. Default 6. round_ll Integer. Rounding (decimal places) applied lon/lat reshaping wide. Default 3 (≈100–120 m grid mid-latitudes). verbose Logical. TRUE, prints progress messages. Default TRUE. attach_to_global Logical. TRUE, assigns output data.frames .GlobalEnv names based file prefix (e.g., weather_esp_lvl2_barcelona_daily). Default FALSE. aggregation_unit Character. Choose "region" (default) produce polygon-wide means, "cell" per-ERA5-cell summaries, "hourly" return raw per-cell hourly series without aggregation. polygon_buffer_km Numeric. ERA5 centroids fall inside admin polygon, expand distance (kilometers) retry. Default 10.

title: Build daily & lagged weather features from processed ERA5 CSVs — process_era5_data
text: aggregation_unit = "region" "cell", (invisibly) list : daily, lags_7d, lags_14d, lags_30d, lags_21d_lag7, ppt_lags, paths (written file paths). aggregation_unit = "hourly", returns list hourly (raw per-cell series) paths.

title: Build daily & lagged weather features from processed ERA5 CSVs — process_era5_data
text: Expects variables CSVs: "10m_u_component_of_wind", "10m_v_component_of_wind" "2m_temperature", "2m_dewpoint_temperature" "surface_pressure", "total_precipitation" Units assumed (ERA5 defaults): temperature Kelvin, precipitation meters per step; wind components m/s.

text: Extends run_brms_model() spatial BYM2 random effect using adjacency matrix derived hex grid. helper accepts either -memory modelling dataset path enriched RDS file construct required adjacency matrix location identifiers supplied. Predictors scaled match baseline specification duplicate observations per grid-date-source-presence combination dropped prior model fitting.

code: run_brms_bym2_model(   dataset = NULL,   formula = NULL,   cellsize_m = 800,   adjacency = NULL,   adjacency_args = list(),   priors = NULL,   nchains = 4,   threads_per_chain = 1,   adapt_delta = 0.995,   max_treedepth = 20,   backend = c("cmdstanr", "rstan"),   iso3 = NULL,   admin_level = NULL,   admin_name = NULL,   write_output = TRUE,   output_path = "data/proc",   input_dir = "data/proc",   save_pars = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Fit a BYM2 Mosquito Alert occupancy model with brms — run_brms_bym2_model
text: dataset -memory modelling dataset (data.frame), bym2_data_prep object, path enriched RDS file. formula Optional character string formula object specifying fixed random effects structure. provided, overrides default base formula. spatial BYM2 term + car(W, gr = <grid_col>, type = "bym2") automatically appended formula. NULL (default), function processes source validation uses set default predictors including sea_days, maxTM_z, ppt_z, ndvi_z, elev_z, pop_z, year, landcover_code. cellsize_m Numeric cell size (meters) hex grid whose adjacency matrix used. Defaults 800, expecting grid_id_800 column dataset. adjacency Optional pre-computed adjacency matrix whose row/column names match grid identifier column. NULL, helper calls build_grid_adjacency(). adjacency_args Named list additional arguments forwarded build_grid_adjacency() adjacency NULL. values override defaults assembled helper. priors Optional brms::set_prior object. NULL, default priors intercepts, coefficients, standard deviations used. nchains Integer. Number MCMC chains. Default 4. threads_per_chain Integer. Number threads per chain within-chain parallelism. adapt_delta Numeric. Target average proposal acceptance probability. Default 0.995. max_treedepth Integer. Max tree depth NUTS. Default 20. backend Character. "cmdstanr" (default) "rstan". iso3, admin_level, admin_name Optional strings locate dataset dataset NULL. write_output Logical. Whether save fitted model disk. output_path, input_dir Paths output/input. save_pars Logical; forwarded brms::brm() save_pars argument. Defaults TRUE retain latent BYM2 parameters. verbose Logical.

title: Fit a BYM2 Mosquito Alert occupancy model with brms — run_brms_bym2_model
